recapPrompt.prompt: Why is the undo step important in backtracking?

choices.label: It makes the next branch start clean
choices.feedback: Correct. Undo removes the old choice so the next branch does not carry extra leftovers.

choices.label: It sorts the answers before saving them
choices.feedback: Not this one. Undo is about resetting the path, not sorting the answers.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain in your own words why some backtracking branches should stop early.

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the backtracking clues
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the main clue for subsets, board path search, and pruning invalid branches.
hints: Word Search marks a cell, explores, then restores it.
Pruning stops branches that are already impossible.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You rebuilt the main backtracking clues from memory.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck subsets, board path undo, and early stopping.

blanks.label: Main clue for board path search
